# Ap

Get AP info


GET/openapi/v1/{omadacId}/sites/{siteId}/aps/{apMac}

produces

\[\
"application/x-www-form-urlencoded"\
\]


consumes

\[\
"\*/\*"\
\]


Note


Get AP info

The interface requires one of the permissions:

Site Device Manager View Only

The possible error code for the interface in the returned body is one of the following error codes (non generic error codes):

-39303 - AP does not exist.

Params


| name | description | in | require | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| omadacId | Omada ID | path | true | string |  |
| siteId | Site ID | path | true | string |  |
| apMac | AP MAC address, like AA-BB-CC-DD-EE-FF | path | true | string |  |

Status


| code | description | schema |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 200 | OK | OperationResponseApOverviewInfo |

Response Params


| name | description | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| errorCode |  | integer(int32) | integer(int32) |
| msg |  | string |  |
| result |  | ApOverviewInfo | ApOverviewInfo |

Response Example


```
{
	"errorCode": 0,
	"msg": "",
	"result": {
		"type": "",
		"mac": "",
		"name": "",
		"ip": "",

			"rssi": 0,
			"snr": 0,
			"txRate": "",
			"txRateInt": 0,
			"rxRate": "",
			"rxRateInt": 0,
			"upBytes": 0,
			"downBytes": 0,
			"upPackets": 0,
			"downPackets": 0,
			"activity": 0,
			"supportSpeedTest": true,
			"model": "",
			"modelVersion": "",
			"ip": "",
			"type": "",
			"uplinkPort": ""
		},
		"model": "",
		"firmwareVersion": "",
		"cpuUtil": 0,
		"memoryUtil": 0,

	}
}
```

Start rf scan


POST/openapi/v1/{omadacId}/sites/{siteId}/aps/{apMac}/start-rf-scan

produces

\[\
"application/x-www-form-urlencoded"\
\]


consumes

\[\
"\*/\*"\
\]


Note


Wi-Fi connection will lost for several minutes during the scanning. Please select a spare time of network to start scanning. This interface has been deprecated. Please use the following interface instead: Start rf scan v2.

The interface requires one of the permissions:

Site Device Manager Modify

The possible error code for the interface in the returned body is one of the following error codes (non generic error codes):

-34800 - No SSIDs are configured on the corresponding radio band.

-34802 - RF scan failed. Please turn on the radio.

-34803 - The AP is performing WLAN optimization. Please try again later.

-34808 - This AP is not connected.

-34809 - This AP does not support RF Scanning.

-34810 - Mesh APs do not support RF Scanning.

Params


| name | description | in | require | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| omadacId | Omada ID | path | true | string |  |
| siteId | Site ID | path | true | string |  |
| apMac | AP MAC address, like AA-BB-CC-DD-EE-FF | path | true | string |  |

Status


| code | description | schema |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 200 | OK | OperationResponseWithoutResult |

Response Params


| name | description | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| errorCode |  | integer(int32) | integer(int32) |
| msg |  | string |  |

Response Example


```
{
	"errorCode": 0,
	"msg": ""
}
```

Site Device Manager View Only

-34808 - This AP is not connected.

| name | description | in | require | type | schema |

Start rf scan v2


POST/openapi/v2/{omadacId}/sites/{siteId}/aps/{apMac}/start-rf-scan

produces

\[\
"application/x-www-form-urlencoded",\
"application/json"\
\]


consumes

\[\
"\*/\*"\
\]


Note


Wi-Fi connection will lost for several minutes during the scanning. Please select a spare time of network to start scanning.

The interface requires one of the permissions:

Site Device Manager Modify

The possible error code for the interface in the returned body is one of the following error codes (non generic error codes):

-34800 - No SSIDs are configured on the corresponding radio band.

-34802 - RF scan failed. Please turn on the radio.

-34803 - The AP is performing WLAN optimization. Please try again later.

-34808 - This AP is not connected.

-34809 - This AP does not support RF Scanning.

-34810 - Mesh APs do not support RF Scanning.

-34824 - Rogue AP scanning... Please wait.

Example


```
{
  "radioIdList": []
}
```

Params


| name | description | in | require | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| omadacId | Omada ID | path | true | string |  |
| siteId | Site ID | path | true | string |  |
| apMac | AP MAC address, like AA-BB-CC-DD-EE-FF | path | true | string |  |
| rFScanCommand | RFScanCommand | body | true | RFScanCommand | RFScanCommand |

Status


| code | description | schema |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 200 | OK | OperationResponseWithoutResult |

Response Params


| name | description | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| errorCode |  | integer(int32) | integer(int32) |
| msg |  | string |  |

Response Example


```
{
	"errorCode": 0,
	"msg": ""
}
```

Site Device Manager View Only

```
}
```

```
{
	"errorCode": 0,
	"msg": "",
	"result": [\

			"id": "",\
			"onuId": "",\
			"port": 0,\
			"portType": 0,\
			"lanPort": "",\
			"name": "",\
			"linkStatus": 0,\
			"linkSpeed": 0,\
			"duplex": 0,\
			"supportVlan": true,\
			"localVlanEnable": true,\
			"localVlanId": 0,\
			"localVlanNetworkId": "",\
			"status": true,\
			"supportPoe": true,\
			"poeOutEnable": true,\
			"poeState": 0,\
			"voipState": 0,\
			"supportVlanOption": true,\
			"supportVlanTagged": true,\
			"supportStatusEnable": true,\
			"supportStatusInform": true,\
			"custom": true,\
			"tagged": "",\
			"untagged": "",\
			"taggedNetworkId": [],\
			"untaggedNetworkId": [],\
			"uplinkPort": true,\
			"supportBandwidthControl": true,\
			"bandwidthControlEnable": true,\
			"ingressRateLimit": {\
				"status": true,\
				"rate": 0\
			},\
			"egressRateLimit": {\
				"status": true,\
				"rate": 0\
			},\

				"tx": 0,\
				"rx": 0\
			}\
		}\
	]
}
```

Batch modify multiple APs port config


POST/openapi/v1/{omadacId}/sites/{siteId}/aps/ports/config

produces

\[\
"application/x-www-form-urlencoded",\
"application/json"\
\]


consumes

\[\
"\*/\*"\
\]


Note


Batch modify multiple APs port config.

The interface requires one of the permissions:

Site Device Manager Modify

The possible error code for the interface in the returned body is one of the following error codes (non generic error codes):

-1 - Something went wrong. Please try again later or contact our technical support.

-39051 - Operation failed. Please try again later.

-39303 - AP does not exist.

-39348 - This AP port vlanId can not be same as the exist default LanNetwork profile.

-39759 - Cannot batch configure AP ports since one or several selected APs do not have a downlink port.

Example


```
{
  "apMacList": [],
  "lanPortList": [],
  "name": "",
  "status": true,
  "localVlanEnable": true,
  "localVlanId": 0,
  "localVlanNetworkId": "",
  "poeOutEnable": true,
  "custom": true,
  "tagged": "",
  "untagged": "",
  "taggedNetworkId": [],
  "untaggedNetworkId": [],
  "bandwidthControlEnable": true,
  "ingressRateLimit": {
    "status": true,
    "rate": 0
  },
  "egressRateLimit": {
    "status": true,
    "rate": 0
  }
}
```

Params


| name | description | in | require | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| omadacId | Omada ID | path | true | string |  |
| siteId | Site ID | path | true | string |  |
| batchUpdateMultiApPortsOpenApiVO | BatchUpdateMultiApPortsOpenApiVO | body | true | BatchUpdateMultiApPortsOpenApiVO | BatchUpdateMultiApPortsOpenApiVO |

Status


| code | description | schema |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 200 | OK | OperationResponseWithoutResult |

Response Params


| name | description | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| errorCode |  | integer(int32) | integer(int32) |
| msg |  | string |  |

Response Example


```
{
	"errorCode": 0,
	"msg": ""
}
```

Start speed test


POST/openapi/v1/{omadacId}/sites/{siteId}/aps/{apMac}/start-speed-test

produces

\[\
"application/x-www-form-urlencoded",\
"application/json"\
\]


consumes

\[\
"\*/\*"\
\]


Note


The speed test is initiated by device \[apMac\]. Different MAC addresses indicates a different speed test.(Only for P2P device)

The interface requires one of the permissions:

Site Device Manager Modify

The possible error code for the interface in the returned body is one of the following error codes (non generic error codes):

-34817 - Failed to start speedTest, the Main AP is not in CONNECTED, The device connection is unstable.

-34818 - Failed to start speedTest, the Client AP is not in CONNECTED, The device connection is unstable.

-39344 - The Main AP already in speed testing.

-39360 - The current AP’s firmware does not support the link speed test. Please update its firmware and try again.

-39362 - Mesh configurations of the device is lost. Please try to forget and then adopt it again.

-39365 - The Client AP’s firmware does not support the link speed test.(From the Main to the Client) Please update its firmware and try again.

-39366 - The Main AP’s firmware does not support the link speed test.(From Client to Main) Please update its firmware and try again.

Example


```
{
  "childMac": ""
}
```

Params


| name | description | in | require | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| omadacId | Omada ID | path | true | string |  |
| siteId | Site ID | path | true | string |  |
| apMac | AP MAC address, like AA-BB-CC-DD-EE-FF | path | true | string |  |
| speedTestCommand | SpeedTestCommand | body | true | SpeedTestCommand | SpeedTestCommand |

Status


| code | description | schema |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 200 | OK | OperationResponseWithoutResult |

Response Params


| name | description | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| errorCode |  | integer(int32) | integer(int32) |
| msg |  | string |  |

Response Example


```
{
	"errorCode": 0,
	"msg": ""
}
```

Get speed test result


GET/openapi/v1/{omadacId}/sites/{siteId}/aps/{apMac}/speed-test-result

produces

\[\
"application/x-www-form-urlencoded"\
\]


consumes

\[\
"\*/\*"\
\]


Note


Get the test results of the speed from device \[apMac\].(Only for P2P device)

The interface requires one of the permissions:

Site Device Manager View Only

The possible error code for the interface in the returned body is one of the following error codes (non generic error codes):

-39360 - The current AP’s firmware does not support the link speed test. Please update its firmware and try again.

Params


| name | description | in | require | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| omadacId | Omada ID | path | true | string |  |
| siteId | Site ID | path | true | string |  |
| apMac | AP MAC address, like AA-BB-CC-DD-EE-FF | path | true | string |  |

Status


| code | description | schema |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 200 | OK | OperationResponseApSpeedTestResultsOpenApiVO |

Response Params


| name | description | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| errorCode |  | integer(int32) | integer(int32) |
| msg |  | string |  |
| result |  | ApSpeedTestResultsOpenApiVO | ApSpeedTestResultsOpenApiVO |

Response Example


```
{
	"errorCode": 0,
	"msg": "",
	"result": {
		"mainTesting": true,
		"sourceAP": "",
		"targetAP": "",
		"speedTestResult": {
			"additionalProperties1": {
				"role": 0,
				"downRate": 0,
				"upRate": 0,
				"time": 0,
				"status": 0,
				"errorCode": 0,
				"errorMsg": ""
			}
		}
	}
}
```

POST/openapi/v1/{omadacId}/sites/{siteId}/aps/change-role

produces

\[\
"application/x-www-form-urlencoded",\
"application/json"\
\]


consumes

"\*/\*"\
\]


Note


Switch p2p AP role from main to client or client to main.

The interface requires one of the permissions:

Site Device Manager Modify

The possible error code for the interface in the returned body is one of the following error codes (non generic error codes):

-33004 - Operation failed because other operations (site copying, restoring, template synchronizing, etc.) are being performed on this site. Please wait and try again later.

-39050 - This device does not exist.

-39352 - The current firmware of the Main AP does not support role changing.

-39354 - Cannot change roles when multiple Client APs are connected to the Main AP.

Example


```
{
  "mainMac": "",
  "clientMac": ""
}
```

| name | description | in | require | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| omadacId | Omada ID | path | true | string |  |
| siteId | Site ID | path | true | string |  |

| code | description | schema |

Modify AP ipv6 setting


PATCH/openapi/v1/{omadacId}/sites/{siteId}/aps/{apMac}/ipv6-setting

produces

\[\
"application/x-www-form-urlencoded",\
"application/json"\
\]


consumes

\[\
"\*/\*"\
\]


Note


Modify AP ipv6 setting, the AP should support ipv6. Ensure the configuration is correct. Otherwise the device may be unable to connect to the Controller and require a factory reset.

The interface requires one of the permissions:

Site Device Manager Modify

The possible error code for the interface in the returned body is one of the following error codes (non generic error codes):

-39303 - AP does not exist.

Example


```
{
  "ipv6Enable": true,
  "mode": "",
  "dynamicIpv6Setting": {
    "dnsMode": 0,
    "priDns": "",
    "sndDns": ""
  },
  "staticIpv6Setting": {
    "ipv6Addr": "",
    "prefixLen": 0,
    "defaultGate": "",
    "priDns": "",
    "sndDns": ""
  }
}
```

Params


| name | description | in | require | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| omadacId | Omada ID | path | true | string |  |
| siteId | Site ID | path | true | string |  |
| apMac | AP MAC address, like AA-BB-CC-DD-EE-FF | path | true | string |  |
| apIPv6Setting | ApIPv6Setting | body | true | ApIPv6Setting | ApIPv6Setting |

Status


| code | description | schema |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 200 | OK | OperationResponseWithoutResult |

Response Params


| name | description | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| errorCode |  | integer(int32) | integer(int32) |
| msg |  | string |  |

Response Example


```
{
	"errorCode": 0,
	"msg": ""
}
```

Get the candidate parent AP of the mesh AP


GET/openapi/v1/{omadacId}/sites/{siteId}/aps/{apMac}/grid/mesh/candiParents

produces

\[\
"application/x-www-form-urlencoded"\
\]


consumes

\[\
"\*/\*"\
\]


Note


Get the candidate parent AP of the mesh AP, call before adoption.

The interface requires one of the permissions:

Site Device Manager View Only

The possible error code for the interface in the returned body is one of the following error codes (non generic error codes):

-39050 - This device does not exist.

-39303 - AP does not exist.

-39321 - The candidate uplink AP does not exist. Please refresh the page and choose another one.

Params


| name | description | in | require | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| omadacId | Omada ID | path | true | string |  |
| siteId | Site ID | path | true | string |  |
| apMac | AP MAC address, like AA-BB-CC-DD-EE-FF | path | true | string |  |
| page | Start page number. Start from 1. | query | true | integer(int32) |  |
| pageSize | Number of entries per page. It should be within the range of 1–1000. | query | true | integer(int32) |  |

Status


| code | description | schema |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 200 | OK | OperationResponseGridVOCandidateParentForAdoptOpenApiVO |

Response Params


| name | description | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| errorCode |  | integer(int32) | integer(int32) |
| msg |  | string |  |
| result |  | GridVOCandidateParentForAdoptOpenApiVO | GridVOCandidateParentForAdoptOpenApiVO |

Response Example


```
{
	"errorCode": 0,
	"msg": "",
	"result": {
		"totalRows": 0,
		"currentPage": 0,
		"currentSize": 0,
		"data": [\
			{\
				"mac": "",\
				"name": "",\
				"linkStatus": 0,\
				"hop": 0,\
				"childsnum": 0,\
				"recommend": true,\
				"recommendRadioId": 0,\
				"multiBandInfo": [\
					{\
						"radioId": 0,\
						"channel": 0,\
						"rssi": 0\
					}\
				]\
			}\
		]
	}
}
```

Move site to target customer


POST/openapi/v1/msp/{mspId}/customers/{customerId}/sites/{siteId}/cmd/aps/move

produces

\[\
"application/x-www-form-urlencoded",\
"application/json"\
\]


consumes

\[\
"\*/\*"\
\]


Note


Move site to target customer

The interface requires one of the permissions:

Site Device Manager Modify

The possible error code for the interface in the returned body is one of the following error codes (non generic error codes):

-33421 - The gateway model in the target site is different from the gateway model configured in the WAN Settings Overrides in the original site.

-39034 - Failed to move this site because the devices are upgrading.

-39035 - Failed to move this site because the devices are rebooting.

-39036 - Failed to move this site because the devices are synchronizing configurations.

-39037 - Failed to move this site because the devices are provisioning.

-39050 - This device does not exist.

-44261 - The device does not support cluster and can only be adopted to its own site.

Example


```
{
  "deviceMacs": [],
  "deviceMac": "",
  "customer": "",
  "site": ""
}
```

Params


| name | description | in | require | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| mspId | MSP ID | path | true | string |  |
| customerId | Customer ID | path | true | string |  |
| siteId | Site ID | path | true | string |  |

produces

"application/x-www-form-urlencoded"\
\]


consumes

"\*/\*"\
\]


Note


Update AP location info use GPS, only support gps function aps can get valid data.

The interface requires one of the permissions:

Site Device Manager Modify

The possible error code for the interface in the returned body is one of the following error codes (non generic error codes):

-39050 - This device does not exist.

-39303 - AP does not exist.

-39358 - The AP failed to get valid location information from the GPS module.

| name | description | in | require | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |

| siteId | Site ID | path | true | string |  |
| apMac | AP MAC address, like AA-BB-CC-DD-EE-FF | path | true | string |  |

Status


| code | description | schema |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 200 | OK | OperationResponseApLocationConfigOpenApiVO |

Response Params


| name | description | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| errorCode |  | integer(int32) | integer(int32) |
| msg |  | string |  |
| result |  | ApLocationConfigOpenApiVO | ApLocationConfigOpenApiVO |

Response Example


```
{
	"errorCode": 0,
	"msg": "",
	"result": {
		"longitude": 0,
		"latitude": 0
	}
}
```

"application/x-www-form-urlencoded",\
"application/json"\
\]


consumes

"\*/\*"\
\]


Note


Modify AP port config

Site Device Manager Modify

The possible error code for the interface in the returned body is one of the following error codes (non generic error codes):

-39050 - This device does not exist.

-39051 - Operation failed. Please try again later.

-39303 - AP does not exist.

-39348 - This AP port vlanId can not be same as the exist default LanNetwork profile.

```
{
  "lanPort": "",
  "name": "",
  "status": true,
  "localVlanEnable": true,
  "localVlanId": 0,
  "localVlanNetworkId": "",
  "poeOutEnable": true,
  "custom": true,
  "tagged": "",
  "untagged": "",
  "taggedNetworkId": [],
  "untaggedNetworkId": [],
  "bandwidthControlEnable": true,
  "ingressRateLimit": {
    "status": true,
    "rate": 0
  },
  "egressRateLimit": {
    "status": true,
    "rate": 0
  }
}
```

Params


| name | description | in | require | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| omadacId | Omada ID | path | true | string |  |
| siteId | Site ID | path | true | string |  |
| apMac | AP MAC address, like AA-BB-CC-DD-EE-FF | path | true | string |  |
| port | ap port | path | true | string |  |
| modify AP LAN Port | Modify AP LAN Port | body | true | Modify AP LAN Port | Modify AP LAN Port |

Status


| code | description | schema |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 200 | OK | OperationResponseWithoutResult |

Response Params


| name | description | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| errorCode |  | integer(int32) | integer(int32) |
| msg |  | string |  |

Response Example


```
{
	"errorCode": 0,
	"msg": ""
}
```

Site Device Manager View Only

| name | description | in | require | type | schema |

```
				"profileOverrideEnable": true,\

}
```

Modify switch general config


PATCH/openapi/v1/{omadacId}/sites/{siteId}/switches/{switchMac}/general-config

produces

\[\
"application/x-www-form-urlencoded",\
"application/json"\
\]


consumes

\[\
"\*/\*"\
\]


Note


Modify switch general config.

The interface requires one of the permissions:

Site Device Manager Modify

The possible error code for the interface in the returned body is one of the following error codes (non generic error codes):

-39700 - Switch does not exist

-39742 - The Agile Series Switch should use the corresponding path.

-40205 - The device has been added to the stack group and related configurations cannot be modified.

Example


```
{
  "name": "",
  "ledSetting": 0,
  "tagIds": [],
  "location": {
    "longitude": 0,
    "latitude": 0,
    "address": "",
    "timestamp": 0
  },
  "jumbo": 0,
  "lagHashAlg": 0,
  "sdm": {
    "inUse": "",
    "templates": [\
      {\
        "name": "",\
        "apps": [\
          {\
            "category": 0,\
            "num": 0\
          }\
        ]\
      }\
    ]
  }
}
```

Params


| name | description | in | require | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| omadacId | Omada ID | path | true | string |  |
| siteId | Site ID | path | true | string |  |
| switchMac | Switch MAC address, like AA-BB-CC-DD-EE-FF | path | true | string |  |
| switchGeneralConfig | After the device is added to the stack group, the interface can only modify the device name. | body | true | SwitchGeneralConfig | SwitchGeneralConfig |

Status


| code | description | schema |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 200 | OK | OperationResponseWithoutResult |

Response Params


| name | description | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| errorCode |  | integer(int32) | integer(int32) |
| msg |  | string |  |

Response Example


```
{
	"errorCode": 0,
	"msg": ""
}
```

```
  },

      {\
        "priority": 0,\
        "vlan": "",\
        "enable": 0\
      }\
    ]
  }
}
```

GET/openapi/v1/{omadacId}/sites/{siteId}/switches/es/{switchMac}/general-config

produces

\[\
"application/x-www-form-urlencoded"\
\]


consumes

\[\
"\*/\*"\
\]


Note


Get Agile Series switch general config.

The interface requires one of the permissions:

Site Device Manager View Only

The possible error code for the interface in the returned body is one of the following error codes (non generic error codes):

-39700 - Switch does not exist

-39743 - The Non-Agile Series switch should use the corresponding path.

Params


| name | description | in | require | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| omadacId | Omada ID | path | true | string |  |
| siteId | Site ID | path | true | string |  |
| switchMac | Switch MAC address, like AA-BB-CC-DD-EE-FF | path | true | string |  |

Status


| code | description | schema |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 200 | OK | OperationResponseEasyManagedSwitchGeneralConfigDetail |

Response Params


| name | description | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| errorCode |  | integer(int32) | integer(int32) |
| msg |  | string |  |
| result |  | EasyManagedSwitchGeneralConfigDetail | EasyManagedSwitchGeneralConfigDetail |

Response Example


```
{
	"errorCode": 0,
	"msg": "",
	"result": {
		"name": "",
		"ledSetting": 0,
		"tagIds": [],
		"jumboEnable": true,
		"location": {
			"longitude": 0,
			"latitude": 0,
			"address": "",
			"timestamp": 0
		},
		"remember": true,
		"supportPowerAlert": true,
		"powerAlertEnable": true
	}
}
```

```
    "latitude": 0,
```

| code | description | schema |

produces

\[\
"application/x-www-form-urlencoded"\
\]


consumes

\[\
"\*/\*"\
\]


Note


Get the networks used on switch's ports and LAGs.

The interface requires one of the permissions:

Site Device Manager View Only

The possible error code for the interface in the returned body is one of the following error codes (non generic error codes):

-39700 - Switch does not exist

Params


| name | description | in | require | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| omadacId | Omada ID | path | true | string |  |
| siteId | Site ID | path | true | string |  |
| switchMac | Switch MAC address, like AA-BB-CC-DD-EE-FF | path | true | string |  |
| page | Start page number. Start from 1. | query | true | integer(int32) |  |
| pageSize | Number of entries per page. It should be within the range of 1–1000. | query | true | integer(int32) |  |

Status


| code | description | schema |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 200 | OK | GridVOOswNetworkBriefInfoOpenApiVO |

Response Params


| name | description | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| totalRows | Total rows of all items. | integer(int64) | integer(int64) |
| currentPage | Current page number. | integer(int32) | integer(int32) |
| currentSize | Number of entries per page. | integer(int32) | integer(int32) |
| data |  | array | OswNetworkBriefInfoOpenApiVO |

Response Example


```
{
	"totalRows": 0,
	"currentPage": 0,
	"currentSize": 0,
	"data": [\
		{\
			"id": "",\
			"name": "",\
			"vlan": 0\
		}\
	]
}
```

Get the switch's ports and LAGs that the network affects


GET/openapi/v1/{omadacId}/sites/{siteId}/switches/{switchMac}/port-lag-networks/{networkId}/vlan/{vlan}

produces

\[\
"application/x-www-form-urlencoded"\
\]


consumes

\[\
"\*/\*"\
\]


Note


Get the switch's ports and LAGs that the network affects.

The interface requires one of the permissions:

Site Device Manager View Only

The possible error code for the interface in the returned body is one of the following error codes (non generic error codes):

-33503 - This network does not exist.

-39700 - Switch does not exist

Params


| name | description | in | require | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| omadacId | Omada ID | path | true | string |  |
| siteId | Site ID | path | true | string |  |
| switchMac | Switch MAC address, like AA-BB-CC-DD-EE-FF | path | true | string |  |
| networkId | Switch network ID. | path | true | string |  |
| vlan | VLAN. | path | true | string |  |

Status


| code | description | schema |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 200 | OK | OswPortAndLagNetworkVO |

Response Params


| name | description | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| nativePorts | Native Vlan Port List, for example \[1, 2\] | array |  |
| nativeStPorts | Native Vlan Standard Port List, for example \["1/0/1", "1/0/2"\] | array |  |
| nativeLags | Native Vlan Lag List, for example \[1, 2\] | array |  |
| tagPorts | Tag Vlan Port List, for example \[1, 2\] | array |  |
| tagStPorts | Tag Vlan Standard Port List, for example \["1/0/1", "1/0/2"\] | array |  |
| tagLags | Tag Vlan Lag List, for example \[1, 2\] | array |  |
| untagPorts | Untag Vlan Port List, for example \[1, 2\] | array |  |
| untagStPorts | Untag Vlan Standard Port List, for example \["1/0/1", "1/0/2"\] | array |  |
| untagLags | Untag Vlan Lag List, for example \[1, 2\] | array |  |
| vlan | Vlan ID | integer(int32) | integer(int32) |
| ipaddr | IP address. | string |  |
| mode | Ip address mode, it should be a value as follows: 0:static 1:dynamic | integer(int32) | integer(int32) |

Response Example


```
{
	"nativePorts": [],
	"nativeStPorts": [],
	"nativeLags": [],
	"tagPorts": [],
	"tagStPorts": [],
	"tagLags": [],
	"untagPorts": [],
	"untagStPorts": [],
	"untagLags": [],
	"vlan": 0,
	"ipaddr": "",
	"mode": 0
}
```

| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| omadacId | Omada ID | path | true | string |  |
| siteId | Site ID | path | true | string |  |
| switchMac | Switch MAC address, like AA-BB-CC-DD-EE-FF | path | true | string |  |

| code | description | schema |

| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| errorCode |  | integer(int32) | integer(int32) |
| msg |  | string |  |
| result |  | array | NetworkPortsAssociationVO |

```
			"name": "",\
```

```
					"priDns": "",\
```

Note


Batch modify switch vlan interfaces.

Site Device Manager Modify

The possible error code for the interface in the returned body is one of the following error codes (non generic error codes):

-33538 - The number of VLAN Interface entries has reached the limit.

-33549 - Only one VLAN interface can be enabled for the switches in a LAN network whose VLAN Type is Multiple.

-39700 - Switch does not exist

```

    {\
      "id": "",\
      "vlan": 0,\
      "status": true\
    }\
  ]
}
```

| batchApplyOswNetworkOpenApi | BatchApplyOswNetworkOpenApi | body | true | BatchApplyOswNetworkOpenApi | BatchApplyOswNetworkOpenApi |

| code | description | schema |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 200 | OK | OperationResponseWithoutResult |

| name | description | type | schema |

| msg |  | string |  |

```
}
```

Modify switch network config.


POST/openapi/v1/{omadacId}/sites/{siteId}/switches/{switchMac}/networks/{networkId}

produces

\[\
"application/x-www-form-urlencoded",\
"application/json"\
\]


consumes

\[\
"\*/\*"\
\]


Note


Modify switch network config.

The interface requires one of the permissions:

Site Device Manager Modify

The possible error code for the interface in the returned body is one of the following error codes (non generic error codes):

-33529 - The network cannot be disabled because it has been configured as the Management VLAN.

-33538 - The number of VLAN Interface entries has reached the limit.

-33549 - Only one VLAN interface can be enabled for the switches in a LAN network whose VLAN Type is Multiple.

-33555 - The IP range you set for the VLAN Interface conflicts with the IP range of another VLAN Interface.

-35207 - IP address is null or invalid.

-39700 - Switch does not exist

-39710 - Management VLAN does not exist.

Example


```
{
  "id": "",
  "vlan": 0,
  "status": true,
  "mvlan": true,
  "name": "",
  "ip": {
    "mode": 0,
    "ip": "",
    "gateway": "",
    "netmask": "",
    "preDns": "",
    "secDns": "",
    "fallback": true,
    "fallbackIp": "",
    "fallbackMask": "",
    "fallbackGate": "",
    "option12": "",
    "useFixedAddr": true,
    "netId": "",
    "dhcpIp": "",
    "serverType": "",
    "serverMac": "",
    "serverStackId": "",
    "confirmConflict": true
  },
  "ipv6Enable": true,
  "ipv6": {
    "mode": "",
    "dnsMode": 0,
    "priDns": "",
    "sndDns": "",
    "ipv6Addr": "",
    "prefixLen": 0
  },
  "mode": 0,
  "dhcpServer": {
    "ip": "",
    "netmask": "",
    "ipRangePool": [\
      {\
        "startIp": "",\
        "endIp": ""\
      }\
    ],
    "priDns": "",
    "sndDns": "",
    "leasetime": 0,
    "gateway": "",
    "option138": "",
    "options": [\
      {\
        "code": 0,\
        "type": 0,\
        "value": ""\
      }\
    ],
    "vrfId": ""
  },
  "dhcpRelay": {
    "addr": "",
    "vrfId": "",
    "serverAddrs": []
  },
  "vrfId": ""
}
```

Params


| name | description | in | require | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| omadacId | Omada ID | path | true | string |  |
| siteId | Site ID | path | true | string |  |
| switchMac | Switch MAC address, like AA-BB-CC-DD-EE-FF | path | true | string |  |
| networkId | Switch network ID. | path | true | string |  |
| oswNetworkOpenApi | OswNetworkOpenApi | body | true | OswNetworkOpenApi | OswNetworkOpenApi |

| code | description | schema |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 200 | OK | OperationResponseWithoutResult |

| name | description | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |

| msg |  | string |  |

```
	"msg": ""
}
```

Get switch ospf neighbor table


GET/openapi/v1/{omadacId}/sites/{siteId}/switches/{switchMac}/ospf-neighbors

produces

\[\
"application/x-www-form-urlencoded"\
\]


consumes

\[\
"\*/\*"\
\]


Note


Get switch ospf neighbor table.

The interface requires one of the permissions:

Site Device Manager View Only

Params


| name | description | in | require | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| omadacId | Omada ID | path | true | string |  |
| siteId | Site ID | path | true | string |  |
| switchMac | Switch MAC address, like AA-BB-CC-DD-EE-FF | path | true | string |  |
| page | Start page number. Start from 1. | query | true | integer(int32) |  |
| pageSize | Number of entries per page. It should be within the range of 1–1000. | query | true | integer(int32) |  |
| sorts.neighborInterface | Sort parameter may be one of asc or desc. Optional parameter. If it is not carried, it means it is not sorted by this field. When there are more than one, the first one takes effect | query | false | string |  |
| filters.neighborInterface | Filter query parameters, support field neighborInterface | query | false | array | string |
| filters.processId | Filter query parameters, support field processId | query | false | string |  |
| searchKey | Fuzzy query parameters, support field neighborIp | query | false | string |  |

Status


| code | description | schema |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 200 | OK | OperationResponseOswOspfNeighborGridVOOswOspfNeighborVO |

Response Params


| name | description | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| errorCode |  | integer(int32) | integer(int32) |
| msg |  | string |  |
| result |  | OswOspfNeighborGridVOOswOspfNeighborVO | OswOspfNeighborGridVOOswOspfNeighborVO |

Response Example


```
{
	"errorCode": 0,
	"msg": "",
	"result": {
		"totalRows": 0,
		"currentPage": 0,
		"currentSize": 0,
		"data": [\
			{\
				"dead": "",\
				"neighborInterface": "",\
				"neighborIp": "",\
				"routerId": "",\
				"priority": 0,\
				"state": "",\
				"processId": ""\
			}\
		],
		"interfaces": [],
		"processes": []
	}
}
```

Move site to target customer


POST/openapi/v1/msp/{mspId}/customers/{customerId}/sites/{siteId}/cmd/switches/move

produces

\[\
"application/x-www-form-urlencoded",\
"application/json"\
\]


consumes

\[\
"\*/\*"\
\]


Note


Move site to target customer

The interface requires one of the permissions:

Site Device Manager Modify

The possible error code for the interface in the returned body is one of the following error codes (non generic error codes):

-33421 - The gateway model in the target site is different from the gateway model configured in the WAN Settings Overrides in the original site.

-39034 - Failed to move this site because the devices are upgrading.

-39035 - Failed to move this site because the devices are rebooting.

-39036 - Failed to move this site because the devices are synchronizing configurations.

-39037 - Failed to move this site because the devices are provisioning.

-39050 - This device does not exist.

-44261 - The device does not support cluster and can only be adopted to its own site.

Example


```
{
  "deviceMacs": [],
  "deviceMac": "",
  "customer": "",
  "site": ""
}
```

Params


| name | description | in | require | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| mspId | MSP ID | path | true | string |  |
| customerId | Customer ID | path | true | string |  |
| siteId | Site ID | path | true | string |  |
| moveToCustomerOpenApiVO | MoveToCustomerOpenApiVO | body | true | MoveToCustomerOpenApiVO | MoveToCustomerOpenApiVO |

Status


| code | description | schema |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 200 | OK | OperationResponseWithoutResult |

Response Params

Clear the switches ports counters


POST/openapi/v1/{omadacId}/sites/{siteId}/switches/multi-ports/clear-counters

produces

\[\
"application/x-www-form-urlencoded",\
"application/json"\
\]


consumes

\[\
"\*/\*"\
\]


Note


Clear the switches ports counters.

The interface requires one of the permissions:

Site Device Manager Modify

Device Config Page Modify

Example


```
{
  "portList": [\
    {\
      "port": 0,\
      "switchMac": ""\
    }\
  ]
}
```

Params


| name | description | in | require | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| omadacId | Omada ID | path | true | string |  |
| siteId | Site ID | path | true | string |  |
| clearCountersVO | ClearCountersVO | body | true | ClearCountersVO | ClearCountersVO |

Status


| code | description | schema |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 200 | OK | OperationResponseString |

Response Params


| name | description | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| errorCode |  | integer(int32) | integer(int32) |
| msg |  | string |  |
| result |  | string |  |

Response Example


```
{
	"errorCode": 0,
	"msg": "",
	"result": ""
}
```

Modify switch port

\[\
"application/x-www-form-urlencoded",\
"application/json"\
\]


consumes

\[\
"\*/\*"\
\]


Note


Modify switch port.

The interface requires one of the permissions:

Site Device Manager Modify

```

```

Params


| name | description | in | require | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| omadacId | Omada ID | path | true | string |  |
| siteId | Site ID | path | true | string |  |
| switchMac | Switch MAC address, like AA-BB-CC-DD-EE-FF | path | true | string |  |
| port | Port ID | path | true | string |  |
| oswPortSettingVO | OswPortSettingVO | body | true | OswPortSettingVO | OswPortSettingVO |

Status


| code | description | schema |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 200 | OK | OperationResponseString |

Response Params


| name | description | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| errorCode |  | integer(int32) | integer(int32) |
| msg |  | string |  |
| result |  | string |  |

Response Example


```
{
	"errorCode": 0,
	"msg": "",
	"result": ""
}
```

produces

"application/x-www-form-urlencoded"\
\]


consumes

\[\
"\*/\*"\
\]


Note

The interface requires one of the permissions:

Site Device Manager View Only

| name | description | in | require | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |

| page | Start page number. Start from 1. | query | true | integer(int32) |  |
| pageSize | Number of entries per page. It should be within the range of 1–1000. | query | true | integer(int32) |  |
| sorts.portId | Sort parameter may be one of asc or desc. Optional parameter. If it is not carried, it means it is not sorted by this field. When there are more than one, the first one takes effect | query | false | string |  |
| searchKey | Fuzzy query parameters, support field portId,standardPort,deviceId,systemName | query | false | string |  |

Status


| code | description | schema |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 200 | OK | OperationResponseGridVOOswLldpNeighborVO |

```
{
	"errorCode": 0,
	"msg": "",
	"result": {
		"totalRows": 0,
		"currentPage": 0,
		"currentSize": 0,
		"data": [\
			{\
				"portId": 0,\
				"standardPort": "",\
				"deviceId": "",\
				"systemName": "",\
```

Create new vrf


POST/openapi/v1/{omadacId}/sites/{siteId}/switches/{switchMac}/vrfs

produces

\[\
"application/x-www-form-urlencoded",\
"application/json"\
\]


consumes

\[\
"\*/\*"\
\]


Note


Create new vrf.

The interface requires one of the permissions:

Site Device Manager Modify

The possible error code for the interface in the returned body is one of the following error codes (non generic error codes):

-39700 - Switch does not exist

-39945 - This switch VRF name is reserved for device management purposes, please use a different name.

-39970 - The number of VRF entries in this switch has reached the limit.

-39971 - This switch VRF Name already exists.

Example


```
{
  "vrf": "",
  "ipv4Enable": true,
  "ipv6Enable": true
}
```

Params


| name | description | in | require | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| omadacId | Omada ID | path | true | string |  |
| siteId | Site ID | path | true | string |  |
| switchMac | Switch MAC address, like AA-BB-CC-DD-EE-FF | path | true | string |  |
| oswVrfConfigOpenApiVO | OswVrfConfigOpenApiVO | body | true | OswVrfConfigOpenApiVO | OswVrfConfigOpenApiVO |

Status


| code | description | schema |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 200 | OK | OperationResponseWithoutResult |

Response Params


| name | description | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| errorCode |  | integer(int32) | integer(int32) |
| msg |  | string |  |

Response Example


```
{
	"errorCode": 0,
	"msg": ""
}
```

Modify vrf


PUT/openapi/v1/{omadacId}/sites/{siteId}/switches/{switchMac}/vrfs/{vrfId}

produces

\[\
"application/x-www-form-urlencoded",\
"application/json"\
\]


consumes

\[\
"\*/\*"\
\]


Note


Modify vrf.

The interface requires one of the permissions:

Site Device Manager Modify

The possible error code for the interface in the returned body is one of the following error codes (non generic error codes):

-39700 - Switch does not exist

-39948 - The default VRF cannot be edited.

-39971 - This switch VRF Name already exists.

-39973 - This VRF is being used by other IPv6 functions and the IPv6 address-family cannot be disabled.

-39975 - Switch VRF does not exist.

Example


```
{
  "vrf": "",
  "ipv4Enable": true,
  "ipv6Enable": true
}
```

Params


| name | description | in | require | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| omadacId | Omada ID | path | true | string |  |
| siteId | Site ID | path | true | string |  |
| switchMac | Switch MAC address, like AA-BB-CC-DD-EE-FF | path | true | string |  |
| vrfId | VRF ID | path | true | string |  |
| oswVrfConfigOpenApiVO | OswVrfConfigOpenApiVO | body | true | OswVrfConfigOpenApiVO | OswVrfConfigOpenApiVO |

Status


| code | description | schema |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 200 | OK | OperationResponseWithoutResult |

Response Params


| name | description | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| errorCode |  | integer(int32) | integer(int32) |
| msg |  | string |  |

Response Example


```
{
	"errorCode": 0,
	"msg": ""
}
```


Get stack remember Config


GET/openapi/v1/{omadacId}/sites/{siteId}/stacks/{stackId}/remember

produces

\[\
"application/x-www-form-urlencoded"\
\]


consumes

\[\
"\*/\*"\
\]


Note


Get stack remember Config.

The interface requires one of the permissions:

Site Device Manager View Only

Params


| name | description | in | require | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| omadacId | Omada ID | path | true | string |  |
| siteId | Site ID | path | true | string |  |
| stackId | Stack ID | path | true | string |  |

Status


| code | description | schema |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 200 | OK | OperationResponseDeviceRememberConfig |

Response Params


| name | description | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| errorCode |  | integer(int32) | integer(int32) |
| msg |  | string |  |
| result |  | DeviceRememberConfig | DeviceRememberConfig |

Response Example


```
{
	"errorCode": 0,
	"msg": "",
	"result": {
		"remember": 0
	}
}
```

Create Switch Stack


POST/openapi/v1/{omadacId}/sites/{siteId}/stacks

produces

\[\
"application/x-www-form-urlencoded",\
"application/json"\
\]


consumes

\[\
"\*/\*"\
\]


Note


Create Switch Stack.

The interface requires one of the permissions:

Site Device Manager Modify

The possible error code for the interface in the returned body is one of the following error codes (non generic error codes):

-34686 - This service port is already in use, please choose another port.

-39093 - The length of the device name exceeds the limit.

-39098 - The number of members exceeds the maximum member limit allowed by the controller.

-39700 - Switch does not exist

-39715 - The member port in the LAG is invalid.

-40201 - Incompatible stack device exists. Please check the stack compatibility.


Params


| name | description | in | require | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| omadacId | Omada ID | path | true | string |  |
| siteId | Site ID | path | true | string |  |
| oswStackConfigOpenApiVO | OswStackConfigOpenApiVO | body | true | OswStackConfigOpenApiVO | OswStackConfigOpenApiVO |

Status


| code | description | schema |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 200 | OK | OperationResponseWithoutResult |

Response Params


| name | description | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| errorCode |  | integer(int32) | integer(int32) |
| msg |  | string |  |

Response Example


```
{
	"errorCode": 0,
	"msg": ""
}
```

Modify stack remember Config


POST/openapi/v1/{omadacId}/sites/{siteId}/stacks/{stackId}/remember

produces

\[\
"application/x-www-form-urlencoded",\
"application/json"\
\]


consumes

\[\
"\*/\*"\
\]


Note


Modify stack remember Config.

The interface requires one of the permissions:

Site Device Manager Modify

Example


```
{
  "remember": 0
}
```

Params


| name | description | in | require | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| omadacId | Omada ID | path | true | string |  |
| siteId | Site ID | path | true | string |  |
| stackId | Stack ID | path | true | string |  |
| deviceRememberConfig | DeviceRememberConfig | body | true | DeviceRememberConfig | DeviceRememberConfig |

Status


| code | description | schema |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 200 | OK | OperationResponseWithoutResult |

Response Params


| name | description | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| errorCode |  | integer(int32) | integer(int32) |
| msg |  | string |  |

Response Example


```
{
	"errorCode": 0,
	"msg": ""
}
```

Modify Switch Stack


PUT/openapi/v1/{omadacId}/sites/{siteId}/stacks/{stackId}

produces

\[\
"application/x-www-form-urlencoded",\
"application/json"\
\]


consumes

\[\
"\*/\*"\
\]


Note


Modify Switch Stack.

The interface requires one of the permissions:

Site Device Manager Modify

The possible error code for the interface in the returned body is one of the following error codes (non generic error codes):

-34686 - This service port is already in use, please choose another port.

-39093 - The length of the device name exceeds the limit.

-39098 - The number of members exceeds the maximum member limit allowed by the controller.

-39101 - The device has been adopted at another site. Please access the site and forget the device first.

-39102 - The primary device is applying configurations. Please adopt the member device later.

-39103 - There are stack unit conflicts.

-39700 - Switch does not exist

-39715 - The member port in the LAG is invalid.

-40201 - Incompatible stack device exists. Please check the stack compatibility.


Params


| name | description | in | require | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| omadacId | Omada ID | path | true | string |  |
| siteId | Site ID | path | true | string |  |
| stackId | Stack ID | path | true | string |  |
| oswStackConfigOpenApiVO | OswStackConfigOpenApiVO | body | true | OswStackConfigOpenApiVO | OswStackConfigOpenApiVO |

Status


| code | description | schema |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 200 | OK | OperationResponseWithoutResult |

Response Params


| name | description | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| errorCode |  | integer(int32) | integer(int32) |
| msg |  | string |  |

Response Example


```
{
	"errorCode": 0,
	"msg": ""
}
```

Get msp stack List


GET/openapi/v1/msp/{mspId}/stack

produces

\[\
"application/x-www-form-urlencoded"\
\]


consumes

\[\
"\*/\*"\
\]


Note


Get msp stack List.

The interface requires one of the permissions:

MSP Device Manager View Only

Params


| name | description | in | require | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| mspId | MSP ID | path | true | string |  |
| page | Start page number. Start from 1. | query | true | integer(int32) |  |
| pageSize | Number of entries per page. It should be within the range of 1–1000. | query | true | integer(int32) |  |

Status


| code | description | schema |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 200 | OK | OperationResponseWithoutResult |

Response Params


| name | description | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| errorCode |  | integer(int32) | integer(int32) |
| msg |  | string |  |

Response Example


```
{
	"errorCode": 0,
	"msg": ""
}
```

DELETE/openapi/v1/{omadacId}/sites/{siteId}/stacks/{stackId}

produces

\[\
"application/x-www-form-urlencoded"\
\]


consumes

\[\
"\*/\*"\
\]


Note


Delete Switch Stack.

The interface requires one of the permissions:

Site Device Manager Modify

Params


| name | description | in | require | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| omadacId | Omada ID | path | true | string |  |
| siteId | Site ID | path | true | string |  |
| stackId | Stack ID | path | true | string |  |

Status


| code | description | schema |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 200 | OK | OperationResponseWithoutResult |

Response Params


| name | description | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| errorCode |  | integer(int32) | integer(int32) |
| msg |  | string |  |

```
	"errorCode": 0,
	"msg": ""
}
```

Get msp stack detail


GET/openapi/v1/msp/{mspId}/customers/{customerId}/sites/{siteId}/stack/{stackId}

produces

\[\
"application/x-www-form-urlencoded"\
\]


consumes

\[\
"\*/\*"\
\]


Note


Get msp stack detail.

The interface requires one of the permissions:

MSP Device Manager View Only

The possible error code for the interface in the returned body is one of the following error codes (non generic error codes):

-40200 - This switch stack is not existed.

Params


| name | description | in | require | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| mspId | MSP ID | path | true | string |  |
| customerId | customerId | path | true | string |  |
| siteId | Site ID | path | true | string |  |
| stackId | Stack ID | path | true | string |  |

Status


| code | description | schema |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 200 | OK | OperationResponseWithoutResult |

Response Params


| name | description | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| errorCode |  | integer(int32) | integer(int32) |
| msg |  | string |  |

Response Example


```
{
	"errorCode": 0,
	"msg": ""
}
```

Create stack staticRouting


POST/openapi/v1/{omadacId}/sites/{siteId}/stacks/{stackId}/staticRoutings

produces

\[\
"application/x-www-form-urlencoded",\
"application/json"\
\]


consumes

\[\
"\*/\*"\
\]


Note


Create stack staticRouting.

The interface requires one of the permissions:

Site Device Manager Modify

The possible error code for the interface in the returned body is one of the following error codes (non generic error codes):

-40200 - This switch stack is not existed.

Example


```
{
  "ipVersion": 0,
  "status": true,
  "destinations": [],
  "nextHopIp": "",
  "distance": 0
}
```

Params


| name | description | in | require | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| omadacId | Omada ID | path | true | string |  |
| siteId | Site ID | path | true | string |  |
| stackId | Stack ID | path | true | string |  |
| oswStaticRoutingConfigOpenApiVO | OswStaticRoutingConfigOpenApiVO | body | true | OswStaticRoutingConfigOpenApiVO | OswStaticRoutingConfigOpenApiVO |

Status


| code | description | schema |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 200 | OK | OperationResponseWithoutResult |

Response Params


| name | description | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| errorCode |  | integer(int32) | integer(int32) |
| msg |  | string |  |

Response Example


```
{
	"errorCode": 0,
	"msg": ""
}
```

Get discovery stack list


GET/openapi/v1/{omadacId}/sites/{siteId}/stacks/discovery

produces

\[\
"application/x-www-form-urlencoded"\
\]


consumes

\[\
"\*/\*"\
\]


Note


Get discovery stack list.

The interface requires one of the permissions:

Site Device Manager View Only

Params


| name | description | in | require | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| omadacId | Omada ID | path | true | string |  |
| siteId | Site ID | path | true | string |  |
| page | Start page number. Start from 1. | query | true | integer(int32) |  |
| pageSize | Number of entries per page. It should be within the range of 1–1000. | query | true | integer(int32) |  |

Status


| code | description | schema |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 200 | OK | OperationResponseWithoutResult |

Response Params


| name | description | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| errorCode |  | integer(int32) | integer(int32) |
| msg |  | string |  |

Response Example


```
{
	"errorCode": 0,
	"msg": ""
}
```

Get grid stack staticRouting


GET/openapi/v1/{omadacId}/sites/{siteId}/stacks/{stackId}/staticRoutings

produces

\[\
"application/x-www-form-urlencoded"\
\]


consumes

\[\
"\*/\*"\
\]


Note


Get grid stack staticRouting.

The interface requires one of the permissions:

Site Device Manager View Only

The possible error code for the interface in the returned body is one of the following error codes (non generic error codes):

-40200 - This switch stack is not existed.

Params


| name | description | in | require | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| omadacId | Omada ID | path | true | string |  |
| siteId | Site ID | path | true | string |  |
| stackId | Stack ID | path | true | string |  |
| page | Start page number. Start from 1. | query | true | integer(int32) |  |
| pageSize | Number of entries per page. It should be within the range of 1–1000. | query | true | integer(int32) |  |

Status


| code | description | schema |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 200 | OK | OperationResponseOswStaticRoutingGridVOOswStaticRoutingVO |

Response Params


| name | description | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| errorCode |  | integer(int32) | integer(int32) |
| msg |  | string |  |
| result |  | OswStaticRoutingGridVOOswStaticRoutingVO | OswStaticRoutingGridVOOswStaticRoutingVO |

Response Example


```
{
	"errorCode": 0,
	"msg": "",
	"result": {
		"totalRows": 0,
		"currentPage": 0,
		"currentSize": 0,
		"data": [\
			{\
				"id": "",\
				"omadacId": "",\
				"siteId": "",\
				"entryId": 0,\
				"name": "",\
				"mac": "",\
				"vrfId": "",\
				"ipVersion": 0,\
				"status": true,\
				"destinations": [],\
				"nextHopIp": "",\
				"nextHopVrfId": "",\
				"distance": 0,\
				"resource": 0\
			}\
		],
		"supportIpv6StaticRouting": true
	}
}
```

Get stackable switches


GET/openapi/v1/{omadacId}/sites/{siteId}/stacks/switches

produces

\[\
"application/x-www-form-urlencoded"\
\]


consumes

\[\
"\*/\*"\
\]


Note


Get stackable switches.

The interface requires one of the permissions:

Site Device Manager View Only

The possible error code for the interface in the returned body is one of the following error codes (non generic error codes):

-40200 - This switch stack is not existed.

Params


| name | description | in | require | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| omadacId | Omada ID | path | true | string |  |
| siteId | Site ID | path | true | string |  |
| stackId | Stack ID | query | true | string |  |

Status


| code | description | schema |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 200 | OK | OperationResponseOswStackSwitchVO |

Response Params


| name | description | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| errorCode |  | integer(int32) | integer(int32) |
| msg |  | string |  |
| result |  | OswStackSwitchVO | OswStackSwitchVO |

Modify stack staticRouting


PUT/openapi/v1/{omadacId}/sites/{siteId}/stacks/{stackId}/staticRoutings/{staticRoutingId}

produces

\[\
"application/x-www-form-urlencoded",\
"application/json"\
\]


consumes

\[\
"\*/\*"\
\]


Note


Modify stack staticRouting.

The interface requires one of the permissions:

Site Device Manager Modify

The possible error code for the interface in the returned body is one of the following error codes (non generic error codes):

-40200 - This switch stack is not existed.

Example


```
{
  "ipVersion": 0,
  "status": true,
  "destinations": [],
  "nextHopIp": "",
  "distance": 0
}
```

Params


| name | description | in | require | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| omadacId | Omada ID | path | true | string |  |
| siteId | Site ID | path | true | string |  |
| stackId | Stack ID | path | true | string |  |
| staticRoutingId | Static routing ID | path | true | string |  |
| oswStaticRoutingConfigOpenApiVO | OswStaticRoutingConfigOpenApiVO | body | true | OswStaticRoutingConfigOpenApiVO | OswStaticRoutingConfigOpenApiVO |

Status


| code | description | schema |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 200 | OK | OperationResponseWithoutResult |

Response Params


| name | description | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| errorCode |  | integer(int32) | integer(int32) |
| msg |  | string |  |

Response Example


```
{
	"errorCode": 0,
	"msg": ""
}
```

Detect switch stack members


POST/openapi/v1/{omadacId}/sites/{siteId}/stacks/detect/{stackId}

produces

\[\
"application/x-www-form-urlencoded"\
\]


consumes

\[\
"\*/\*"\
\]


Note


Detect switch stack members.

The interface requires one of the permissions:

Site Device Manager Modify

The possible error code for the interface in the returned body is one of the following error codes (non generic error codes):

-40200 - This switch stack is not existed.

Params


| name | description | in | require | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| omadacId | Omada ID | path | true | string |  |
| siteId | Site ID | path | true | string |  |
| stackId | Stack ID | path | true | string |  |

Status


| code | description | schema |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 200 | OK | OperationResponseWithoutResult |

Response Params


| name | description | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| errorCode |  | integer(int32) | integer(int32) |
| msg |  | string |  |

Response Example


```
{
	"errorCode": 0,
	"msg": ""
}
```

Get stack detail


GET/openapi/v1/{omadacId}/sites/{siteId}/stacks/{stackId}

produces

\[\
"application/x-www-form-urlencoded"\
\]


consumes

\[\
"\*/\*"\
\]


Note


Get stack detail.

The interface requires one of the permissions:

Site Device Manager View Only

The possible error code for the interface in the returned body is one of the following error codes (non generic error codes):

-40200 - This switch stack is not existed.

Params


| name | description | in | require | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| omadacId | Omada ID | path | true | string |  |
| siteId | Site ID | path | true | string |  |
| stackId | Stack ID | path | true | string |  |

Status


| code | description | schema |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 200 | OK | OperationResponseOswStackDetailVO |

| name | description | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |

| msg |  | string |  |
| result |  | OswStackDetailVO | OswStackDetailVO |

Reboot switch stack


POST/openapi/v1/{omadacId}/sites/{siteId}/cmd/stacks/{stackId}/reboot

produces

\[\
"application/x-www-form-urlencoded",\
"application/json"\
\]


consumes

\[\
"\*/\*"\
\]


Note


Reboot switch stack.

The interface requires one of the permissions:

Site Device Manager Modify

The possible error code for the interface in the returned body is one of the following error codes (non generic error codes):

-39012 - Failed to send the reboot command to the device.

-40203 - The primary device is not connected, cannot reboot.

Example


```
{
  "selectAll": true,
  "macs": []
}
```

Params


| name | description | in | require | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| omadacId | Omada ID | path | true | string |  |
| siteId | Site ID | path | true | string |  |
| stackId | Stack ID | path | true | string |  |
| oswStackRebootOpenApiVO | OswStackRebootOpenApiVO | body | true | OswStackRebootOpenApiVO | OswStackRebootOpenApiVO |

Status


| code | description | schema |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 200 | OK | OperationResponseListRebootResultVO |

Response Params


| name | description | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| errorCode |  | integer(int32) | integer(int32) |
| msg |  | string |  |
| result |  | array | RebootResultVO |

Response Example


```
{
	"errorCode": 0,
	"msg": "",
	"result": [\
		{\
			"mac": "",\
			"status": 0,\
			"statusCategory": 0\
		}\
	]
}
```

Locate switch stack


POST/openapi/v1/{omadacId}/sites/{siteId}/cmd/stacks/{stackId}/locate

produces

\[\
"application/x-www-form-urlencoded",\
"application/json"\
\]


consumes

\[\
"\*/\*"\
\]


Note


Locate switch stack.

The interface requires one of the permissions:

Site Device Manager Modify

Site Clients Manager Modify

The possible error code for the interface in the returned body is one of the following error codes (non generic error codes):

-40204 - The primary device is not connected, cannot locate.

Example


```
{
  "selectAll": true,
  "macs": [],
  "locateEnable": true,
  "standardPorts": []
}
```

Params


| name | description | in | require | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| omadacId | Omada ID | path | true | string |  |
| siteId | Site ID | path | true | string |  |
| stackId | Stack ID | path | true | string |  |
| oswStackLocateOpenApiVO | OswStackLocateOpenApiVO | body | true | OswStackLocateOpenApiVO | OswStackLocateOpenApiVO |

Status


| code | description | schema |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 200 | OK | OperationResponseLocateResultVO |

Response Params


| name | description | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| errorCode |  | integer(int32) | integer(int32) |
| msg |  | string |  |
| result |  | LocateResultVO | LocateResultVO |

Response Example


```
{
	"errorCode": 0,
	"msg": "",
	"result": {
		"mac": "",
		"locateEnable": true,
		"restTime": 0
	}
}
```

Modify stack detail


PATCH/openapi/v1/{omadacId}/sites/{siteId}/stacks/{stackId}/config

produces

\[\
"application/x-www-form-urlencoded",\
"application/json"\
\]


consumes

\[\
"\*/\*"\
\]


Note


Modify stack detail.

The interface requires one of the permissions:

Site Device Manager Modify

The possible error code for the interface in the returned body is one of the following error codes (non generic error codes):

-40200 - This switch stack is not existed.

Example


```
{
  "name": "",
  "ledSetting": 0,
  "mvlanNetworkId": "",
  "mvlanBridgeVlan": 0,
  "loopbackDetectEnable": true,
  "stp": 0,
  "snmp": {
    "location": "",
    "contact": ""
  },
  "priority": 0,
  "helloTime": 0,
  "maxAge": 0,
  "forwardDelay": 0,
  "txHoldCount": 0,
  "maxHops": 0,
  "mstp": {
    "instances": [\
      {\
        "priority": 0,\
        "vlan": "",\
        "enable": 0\
      }\
    ]
  },
  "tagIds": [],
  "jumbo": 0,
  "lagHashAlg": 0,
  "location": {
    "longitude": 0,
    "latitude": 0,
    "address": "",
    "timestamp": 0
  },
  "qosConfig": {
    "dscpDot1pMappingId": "",
    "dscpDot1pMappingName": "",
    "dot1pQueueMappingId": "",
    "dot1pQueueMappingName": "",
    "queueSchedulerId": "",
    "queueSchedulerName": ""
  },
  "rememberDevice": 0
}
```

Params


| name | description | in | require | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| omadacId | Omada ID | path | true | string |  |
| siteId | Site ID | path | true | string |  |
| stackId | Stack ID | path | true | string |  |
| oswStackDetailConfigOpenApiVO | OswStackDetailConfigOpenApiVO | body | true | OswStackDetailConfigOpenApiVO | OswStackDetailConfigOpenApiVO |

Status


| code | description | schema |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 200 | OK | OperationResponseWithoutResult |

Response Params


| name | description | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| errorCode |  | integer(int32) | integer(int32) |
| msg |  | string |  |

Response Example


```
{
	"errorCode": 0,
	"msg": ""
}
```

Get the networks detail (including the vlan related ports and lags info) used on stack's ports and LAGs


GET/openapi/v1/{omadacId}/sites/{siteId}/stacks/{stackId}/networks-detail

produces

\[\
"application/x-www-form-urlencoded"\
\]


consumes

\[\
"\*/\*"\
\]


Note


Get the networks detail (including the vlan related ports and lags info) used on stack's ports and LAGs.

The interface requires one of the permissions:

Site Device Manager View Only

The possible error code for the interface in the returned body is one of the following error codes (non generic error codes):

-40200 - This switch stack is not existed.

Params


| name | description | in | require | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| omadacId | Omada ID | path | true | string |  |
| siteId | Site ID | path | true | string |  |
| stackId | Stack ID | path | true | string |  |
| page | Start page number. Start from 1. | query | true | integer(int32) |  |
| pageSize | Number of entries per page. It should be within the range of 1–1000. | query | true | integer(int32) |  |

Status


| code | description | schema |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 200 | OK | GridVOOswNetworkDetailInfoOpenApiVO |

Response Params


| name | description | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| totalRows | Total rows of all items. | integer(int64) | integer(int64) |
| currentPage | Current page number. | integer(int32) | integer(int32) |
| currentSize | Number of entries per page. | integer(int32) | integer(int32) |
| data |  | array | OswNetworkDetailInfoOpenApiVO |

Response Example


```
{
	"totalRows": 0,
	"currentPage": 0,
	"currentSize": 0,
	"data": [\
		{\
			"id": "",\
			"name": "",\
			"vlan": 0,\
			"isolation": true,\
			"portLagNetworkInfo": {\
				"nativePorts": [],\
				"nativeStPorts": [],\
				"nativeLags": [],\
				"tagPorts": [],\
				"tagStPorts": [],\
				"tagLags": [],\
				"untagPorts": [],\
				"untagStPorts": [],\
				"untagLags": [],\
				"vlan": 0,\
				"ipaddr": "",\
				"mode": 0\
			}\
		}\
	]
}
```

```
					"slot": 0,\
					"port": 0\
				},\

}
```

Get stack port List

produces

\[\
"application/x-www-form-urlencoded"\
\]


consumes

\[\
"\*/\*"\
\]


Note


Get stack port List.

The interface requires one of the permissions:

Site Device Manager View Only

The possible error code for the interface in the returned body is one of the following error codes (non generic error codes):

-40200 - This switch stack is not existed.

Params


| name | description | in | require | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| omadacId | Omada ID | path | true | string |  |
| siteId | Site ID | path | true | string |  |
| stackId | Stack ID | path | true | string |  |

Status


| code | description | schema |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 200 | OK | OperationResponseListOswStackPortVO |

Response Params


| name | description | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| errorCode |  | integer(int32) | integer(int32) |
| msg |  | string |  |
| result |  | array | OswStackPortVO |

Get stack cable test logs


GET/openapi/v1/{omadacId}/sites/{siteId}/cable-test/stacks/{stackId}/logs

produces

\[\
"application/x-www-form-urlencoded"\
\]


consumes

\[\
"\*/\*"\
\]


Note


Get stack cable test logs.

The interface requires one of the permissions:

Site Device Manager View Only

The possible error code for the interface in the returned body is one of the following error codes (non generic error codes):

-40200 - This switch stack is not existed.

Params


| name | description | in | require | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| omadacId | Omada ID | path | true | string |  |
| siteId | Site ID | path | true | string |  |
| stackId | Stack ID | path | true | string |  |

Status


| code | description | schema |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 200 | OK | OperationResponseListOswCableTestLogOpenApiVO |

Response Params


| name | description | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| errorCode |  | integer(int32) | integer(int32) |
| msg |  | string |  |
| result |  | array | OswCableTestLogOpenApiVO |

Response Example


```
{
	"errorCode": 0,
	"msg": "",
	"result": [\
		{\
			"type": 0,\
			"timeStamp": 0,\
			"testPortList": [\
				{\
					"port": 0,\
					"standardPort": {\
						"unit": 0,\
						"slot": 0,\
						"port": 0\
					}\
				}\
			],\
			"completePortList": [\
				{}\
			],\
			"cancelPortList": [\
				{}\
			],\
			"timeoutPortList": [\
				{}\
			]\
		}\
	]
}
```

Start stack cable test


POST/openapi/v1/{omadacId}/sites/{siteId}/cable-test/stacks/{stackId}/start

produces

\[\
"application/x-www-form-urlencoded",\
"application/json"\
\]


consumes

\[\
"\*/\*"\
\]


Note


Start stack cable test.

The interface requires one of the permissions:

Site Device Manager Modify

The possible error code for the interface in the returned body is one of the following error codes (non generic error codes):

-39701 - This port does not exist

-39753 - This switch does not support the cable test.

-39754 - SFP port cannot be selected.

-39755 - Disabled port cannot be selected.

-39756 - Uplink port cannot be selected.

-39757 - This switch is running a cable test.

-39810 - Stacking port cannot be selected.

-40200 - This switch stack is not existed.

Example


```
{
  "portList": [\
    {\
      "port": 0,\
      "standardPort": {\
        "unit": 0,\
        "slot": 0,\
        "port": 0\
      }\
    }\
  ]
}
```

Params


| name | description | in | require | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| omadacId | Omada ID | path | true | string |  |
| siteId | Site ID | path | true | string |  |
| stackId | Stack ID | path | true | string |  |
| oswCableTestTestingPortOpenApiVO | OswCableTestTestingPortOpenApiVO | body | true | OswCableTestTestingPortOpenApiVO | OswCableTestTestingPortOpenApiVO |

Status


| code | description | schema |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 200 | OK | OperationResponseWithoutResult |

Response Params


| name | description | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| errorCode |  | integer(int32) | integer(int32) |
| msg |  | string |  |

Response Example


```
{
	"errorCode": 0,
	"msg": ""
}
```

Get the stack's ports and LAGs that the network affects


GET/openapi/v1/{omadacId}/sites/{siteId}/stacks/{stackId}/port-lag-networks/{networkId}/vlan/{vlan}

produces

\[\
"application/x-www-form-urlencoded"\
\]


consumes

\[\
"\*/\*"\
\]


Note


Get the stack's ports and LAGs that the network affects.

The interface requires one of the permissions:

Site Device Manager View Only

The possible error code for the interface in the returned body is one of the following error codes (non generic error codes):

-33503 - This network does not exist.

-40200 - This switch stack is not existed.

Params


| name | description | in | require | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| omadacId | Omada ID | path | true | string |  |
| siteId | Site ID | path | true | string |  |
| stackId | Stack ID | path | true | string |  |
| networkId | Switch network ID. | path | true | string |  |
| vlan | VLAN. | path | true | string |  |

Status


| code | description | schema |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 200 | OK | OswPortAndLagNetworkVO |

Response Params


| name | description | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| nativePorts | Native Vlan Port List, for example \[1, 2\] | array |  |
| nativeStPorts | Native Vlan Standard Port List, for example \["1/0/1", "1/0/2"\] | array |  |
| nativeLags | Native Vlan Lag List, for example \[1, 2\] | array |  |
| tagPorts | Tag Vlan Port List, for example \[1, 2\] | array |  |
| tagStPorts | Tag Vlan Standard Port List, for example \["1/0/1", "1/0/2"\] | array |  |
| tagLags | Tag Vlan Lag List, for example \[1, 2\] | array |  |
| untagPorts | Untag Vlan Port List, for example \[1, 2\] | array |  |
| untagStPorts | Untag Vlan Standard Port List, for example \["1/0/1", "1/0/2"\] | array |  |
| untagLags | Untag Vlan Lag List, for example \[1, 2\] | array |  |
| vlan | Vlan ID | integer(int32) | integer(int32) |
| ipaddr | IP address. | string |  |
| mode | Ip address mode, it should be a value as follows: 0:static 1:dynamic | integer(int32) | integer(int32) |

Response Example


```
{
	"nativePorts": [],
	"nativeStPorts": [],
	"nativeLags": [],
	"tagPorts": [],
	"tagStPorts": [],
	"tagLags": [],
	"untagPorts": [],
	"untagStPorts": [],
	"untagLags": [],
	"vlan": 0,
	"ipaddr": "",
	"mode": 0
}
```

Get stack ospf neighbor table


GET/openapi/v1/{omadacId}/sites/{siteId}/stack/{stackId}/ospf-neighbors

produces

\[\
"application/x-www-form-urlencoded"\
\]


consumes

\[\
"\*/\*"\
\]


Note


Get stack ospf neighbor table.

The interface requires one of the permissions:

Site Device Manager View Only

Params


| name | description | in | require | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| omadacId | Omada ID | path | true | string |  |
| siteId | Site ID | path | true | string |  |
| stackId | Stack ID | path | true | string |  |
| page | Start page number. Start from 1. | query | true | integer(int32) |  |
| pageSize | Number of entries per page. It should be within the range of 1–1000. | query | true | integer(int32) |  |
| sorts.neighborInterface | Sort parameter may be one of asc or desc. Optional parameter. If it is not carried, it means it is not sorted by this field. When there are more than one, the first one takes effect | query | false | string |  |
| filters.neighborInterface | Filter query parameters, support field neighborInterface | query | false | array | string |
| filters.processId | Filter query parameters, support field processId | query | false | string |  |
| searchKey | Fuzzy query parameters, support field neighborIp | query | false | string |  |

Status


| code | description | schema |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 200 | OK | OperationResponseOswOspfNeighborGridVOOswOspfNeighborVO |

Response Params


| name | description | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| errorCode |  | integer(int32) | integer(int32) |
| msg |  | string |  |
| result |  | OswOspfNeighborGridVOOswOspfNeighborVO | OswOspfNeighborGridVOOswOspfNeighborVO |

Response Example


```
{
	"errorCode": 0,
	"msg": "",
	"result": {
		"totalRows": 0,
		"currentPage": 0,
		"currentSize": 0,
		"data": [\
			{\
				"dead": "",\
				"neighborInterface": "",\
				"neighborIp": "",\
				"routerId": "",\
				"priority": 0,\
				"state": "",\
				"processId": ""\
			}\
		],
		"interfaces": [],
		"processes": []
	}
}
```

Create new stack vrf


POST/openapi/v1/{omadacId}/sites/{siteId}/stacks/{stackId}/vrfs

produces

\[\
"application/x-www-form-urlencoded",\
"application/json"\
\]


consumes

\[\
"\*/\*"\
\]


Note


Create new stack vrf

The interface requires one of the permissions:

Site Device Manager Modify

The possible error code for the interface in the returned body is one of the following error codes (non generic error codes):

-39700 - Switch does not exist

-39970 - The number of VRF entries in this switch has reached the limit.

-39971 - This switch VRF Name already exists.

-40200 - This switch stack is not existed.

Example


```
{
  "id": "",
  "vrf": "",
  "ipv4Enable": true,
  "ipv6Enable": true
}
```

Params


| name | description | in | require | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| omadacId | Omada ID | path | true | string |  |
| siteId | Site ID | path | true | string |  |
| stackId | Stack ID | path | true | string |  |
| oswVrfVO | OswVrfVO | body | true | OswVrfVO | OswVrfVO |

Status


| code | description | schema |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 200 | OK | OperationResponseWithoutResult |

Response Params


| name | description | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| errorCode |  | integer(int32) | integer(int32) |
| msg |  | string |  |

Response Example


```
{
	"errorCode": 0,
	"msg": ""
}
```

Modify an existing stack vrf


PUT/openapi/v1/{omadacId}/sites/{siteId}/stacks/{stackId}/vrfs/{vrfId}

produces

\[\
"application/x-www-form-urlencoded",\
"application/json"\
\]


consumes

\[\
"\*/\*"\
\]


Note


Modify an existing stack vrf

The interface requires one of the permissions:

Site Device Manager Modify

The possible error code for the interface in the returned body is one of the following error codes (non generic error codes):

-39700 - Switch does not exist

-39948 - The default VRF cannot be edited.

-39971 - This switch VRF Name already exists.

-39973 - This VRF is being used by other IPv6 functions and the IPv6 address-family cannot be disabled.

-39975 - Switch VRF does not exist.

-40200 - This switch stack is not existed.

Example


```
{
  "ipv6Enable": true
}
```

Params


| name | description | in | require | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| omadacId | Omada ID | path | true | string |  |
| siteId | Site ID | path | true | string |  |
| stackId | Stack ID | path | true | string |  |
| vrfId | VRF ID | path | true | string |  |
| modifyOswVrfOpenApiVO | ModifyOswVrfOpenApiVO | body | true | ModifyOswVrfOpenApiVO | ModifyOswVrfOpenApiVO |

Status


| code | description | schema |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 200 | OK | OperationResponseWithoutResult |

Response Params


| name | description | type | schema |

Note


Delete an existing stack vrf

The interface requires one of the permissions:

Site Device Manager Modify

The possible error code for the interface in the returned body is one of the following error codes (non generic error codes):

-39700 - Switch does not exist

-39947 - The default VRF cannot be deleted.

-39972 - This VRF in use cannot be deleted.

-39975 - Switch VRF does not exist.

-40200 - This switch stack is not existed.

| vrfId | VRF ID | path | true | string |  |

| msg |  | string |  |

consumes

"\*/\*"\
\]

Create M-LAG group.

The interface requires one of the permissions:

Site Device Manager Modify

The possible error code for the interface in the returned body is one of the following error codes (non generic error codes):

-39700 - Switch does not exist

-40058 - This switch does not support MLAG.

-40069 - This MLAG Group Name already exists.

```
  "groupId": "",
  "name": "",

      "dadEnable": true,\

      "dadLocalIp": "",\
      "dadLocalIpv6": "",\
      "dadPeerIp": "",\
      "dadPeerIpv6": "",\
      "peerLinkPorts": []\
    }\
  ]
}
```

| siteId | Site ID | path | true | string |  |
| mlagConfigOpenApiVO | MlagConfigOpenApiVO | body | true | MlagConfigOpenApiVO | MlagConfigOpenApiVO |

| code | description | schema |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 200 | OK | OperationResponseWithoutResult |

| name | description | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| errorCode |  | integer(int32) | integer(int32) |
| msg |  | string |  |

```
	"msg": ""
}
```

"application/x-www-form-urlencoded"\
\]


consumes

"\*/\*"\
\]


Note

The interface requires one of the permissions:

Site Device Manager View Only

Params


| name | description | in | require | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| omadacId | Omada ID | path | true | string |  |
| siteId | Site ID | path | true | string |  |
| mlagId | M-LAG group ID | path | true | string |  |
| type | The level of configuration consistency check should be a value as follows: 0:Critical & Significant 1: Critical; 2: Significant | path | true | string |  |

Status


| code | description | schema |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 200 | OK | OperationResponseMapStringOswMlagCccVO |

Response Params


| name | description | type | schema |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| errorCode |  | integer(int32) | integer(int32) |
| msg |  | string |  |
| result |  | OswMlagCccVO | OswMlagCccVO |

```
					"pathType": 0,\
					"type": 0,\
					"cccRslt": "",\
					"localVal": "",\
					"peerVal": ""\

			]
		}
	}
}
```
